L1.area: Data value submission and type conversion utilities

L2.description: An arbitrary code execution vulnerability exists where user-controlled string values within JSON data are dynamically evaluated as executable code without proper sanitization. When processing data from untrusted sources, specially crafted string values that resemble function definitions are passed directly to a dynamic code evaluation mechanism, allowing execution of arbitrary JavaScript in the browser context.

L3.area: Default value parsing logic for submitted JSON string values
L3.description: An arbitrary code execution vulnerability exists where the default value parser dynamically evaluates string values that begin with a function keyword as executable JavaScript code using an unsafe dynamic evaluation call. When the editable JSON tree component displays or processes data from untrusted sources without a custom value parser callback, attacker-controlled strings can trigger arbitrary code execution in the browser context.

ground_truth.reason: The default value parser uses eval() to execute any string beginning with 'function' as JavaScript code, enabling arbitrary code execution when processing untrusted JSON data.
